blocks.content: Callaway Park turned into a different place at night. In the daytime it was just a park — the big oak at the center, the concrete paths, the fountain that worked about half the time.
They got there early enough to claim a good spot on the grass. Eli had brought a blanket that was too small for four people, which he didn't mention until they were already setting up.

So Eli and Savannah had the blanket and Kit and Daisy sat on the grass alongside it, which was still warm from the day and smelled like something the summer had been building all year. The park filled up around them — families with lawn chairs, other kids from school, couples on their own blankets closer to the screen.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: They're showing Singin' in the Rain.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Is that the one with the—

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I don't know who that is.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I've never actually made it to the end.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I've also never made it to the end of any movie I've tried to watch at home after nine o'clock.

The sky was doing something impressive — the pink and gold that came in around eight, the way it made every tree in the park look like something out of a photograph somebody's parent kept in a drawer.
